title: piscidia communis
intro: Piscidia communis, also known as Bitterwood or Fish Fingers, is a tropical tree native to the Caribbean and Central America, belonging to the Fabaceae family. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, due to its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Piscidia communis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various infections, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Piscidia communis is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in traditional medicine to create tinctures, salves, and ointments for topical application.
